# Exobrain — Personal Knowledge Base

<!-- Auto-generated by codebase-summary. Re-run to update. -->

A personal knowledge base system that ingests learning materials from multiple sources, converts them to unified Markdown, uses AI agents to continuously refine content, and provides spaced-repetition quizzing for long-term retention.

## Core Idea

```
Multiple sources → Ingest Pipeline → Unified Markdown → AI Agent refine → Reviewable & quizzable assets
```

**Learning methodology**: T-shaped learning + Feynman Technique + Spaced Repetition (SM-2) + Active Recall + Just-in-Time Learning.

## Status

🚧 **Design phase** — specification documents are complete, implementation has not started.

## How It Works

### Four-Stage Learning Flow

For any new topic (GitHub repo, book, course, podcast):

1. **Map Building** — AI reads the source and produces a knowledge map (5-10 core concepts, prerequisites)
2. **Breadth Scan** — 3-5 sentence summary per concept + 1-2 quiz questions each
3. **Depth on Demand** — Deep dive into topics you pick, with application questions
4. **Review Schedule** — Wrong answers enter SM-2 spaced repetition queue (1d → 3d → 1w → ...)

### Draft-then-Promote Workflow

AI never writes directly to your knowledge base. All AI output goes to `_drafts/` first. You review, then promote to `concepts/` — ensuring you retain editorial control.

## Supported Sources

| Source | Ingest Script | How It Works |
|--------|--------------|--------------|
| YouTube video | `ingest-youtube.sh` | Downloads subtitles (or transcribes via Whisper API) → Markdown |
| PDF document | `ingest-pdf.sh` | Extracts text via pdftotext/Marker → Markdown |
| GitHub repo | `ingest-deepwiki.sh` | Fetches structured wiki via DeepWiki → Markdown |
| Web article | `ingest-article.js` | Extracts content via Readability + Turndown → Markdown |
| Podcast | `ingest-podcast.sh` | Transcribes audio via Whisper API → Markdown |
| epub book | `ingest-book.py` | Parses chapters via ebooklib → per-chapter Markdown |

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Pipeline orchestration | Bash |
| Core logic | Python |
| Web content extraction | Node.js |
| Data format | Markdown + YAML frontmatter + JSON |
| Speech-to-text | OpenAI Whisper API |
| External tools | yt-dlp, pdftotext/Marker, deepwiki-to-md |

## Project Structure

```
study-vault/
├── personal-knowledge-base-design-v2.md   # Design spec (Traditional Chinese)
├── .kiro/specs/personal-knowledge-base/
│   ├── requirements.md                    # 22 requirements with acceptance criteria
│   ├── design.md                          # Architecture & interfaces
│   └── tasks.md                           # Implementation plan (11 task groups)
├── AGENTS.md                              # AI agent context file
└── .agents/summary/                       # Generated documentation
    ├── index.md                           # Documentation entry point
    ├── architecture.md                    # System design
    ├── components.md                      # Component details
    ├── interfaces.md                      # API contracts
    ├── data_models.md                     # Data schemas
    ├── workflows.md                       # Process flow diagrams
    └── dependencies.md                    # External dependencies
```

### Planned Knowledge Base Structure (after implementation)

```
_inbox/       → Staging area for new sources
_drafts/      → AI-generated drafts awaiting review
concepts/     → Promoted knowledge assets
sources/      → Processed source materials (by type)
quiz/         → SM-2 scheduled quiz bank (bank.json)
_index/       → Auto-generated indexes
topics/       → Cross-concept learning paths
_scripts/     → Automation scripts + AI prompts
```

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.x
- Node.js
- [yt-dlp](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp) — YouTube subtitle/audio download
- [pdftotext](https://poppler.freedesktop.org/) or [Marker](https://github.com/VikParuchuri/marker) — PDF extraction
- [deepwiki-to-md](https://github.com/nicobailon/deepwiki-to-md) — DeepWiki content download
- OpenAI API key (for Whisper transcription) — set `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var

## Getting Started

> Implementation has not started yet. The following describes the planned workflow.

```bash
# 1. Initialize the knowledge base
./_scripts/init-kb.sh

# 2. Ingest a source
./_scripts/ingest-youtube.sh https://youtube.com/watch?v=...

# 3. Run the new-source prompt (via AI agent) to generate drafts

# 4. Review drafts in _drafts/, then promote to concepts/

# 5. Take a quiz
python _scripts/quiz_cli.py --count 10
```
